data.title: Thomson View Condominium to relaunch at a lower reserve price of $808m
data.description:
The property had an initial reserve price of $918m.

Edmund Tie & Company has relaunched the collective sale of Thomson View Condominium via tender.

Zoned “Residential”, the property has a plot ratio of 2.1, which can yield a total gross floor area of approximately 112,792 sq m or 1,240 residential units, based on an average apartment size of 85 sq m.

The property is located next to Upper Thomson Mass Rapid Transit station. It comprises 255 units, including 54 townhouses and a 29-storey block housing a shop unit and 200 apartments. 

It is also near MRT stations, schools, retail shops, eateries, and lifestyle amenities such as MacRitchie Reservoir Park, Bishan Park, and the Singapore Island Country Club.

A supplemental agreement is seeking to lower the reserve price from $918m to $808m, subject to 80% of the owners by strata area and share value signing the agreement. Currently, 65% of the owners have signed.

At $808m, the land rate translates to approximately $1,176 per sq ft (psf) per plot ratio. With an additional 8% bonus floor area, the land rate adjusts to $1,141 psf per plot ratio.

The tender closes on 25 October at 3 pm.

data.title: Woodside and Keppel sign liquid hydrogen supply deal for Singapore data centres
data.description:
This follows their non-binding Heads of Agreement in April 2023.

Australian energy company Woodside and Singapore's Keppel Corporation have signed a conditional Offtake Term Sheet for the supply of liquid hydrogen to power Keppel’s data centres in Singapore. 

The Term Sheet builds on a non-binding Heads of Agreement signed by the two companies in April 2023. It outlines key commercial principles that could lead to a binding offtake agreement as early as 2030, with Woodside's proposed production facilities, such as H2Perth, being potential sources of the liquid hydrogen.

Keppel plans to integrate the hydrogen supply into a broader, long-term, lower-carbon energy portfolio to support its assets.

Woodside CEO Meg O'Neill called the agreement a "significant milestone" in advancing hydrogen opportunities, whilst Keppel Data Centres CEO Wong Wai Meng said the deal strengthens their efforts to ensure a stable, lower-carbon energy supply for their facilities.

The agreement is still subject to further negotiations, approvals, and the signing of a full Sales and Purchase Agreement.

data.title: Shell pipeline leaks oil into Singapore’s Bukom Island
data.description:
Clean-up operations commenced following the detection of the spill.

An oil leak from a Shell land-based pipeline between Bukom Island and Bukom Kecil was detected on 20 October at around 5:30 am.

Later that day, the Maritime and Port Authority of Singapore (MPA) was alerted to the incident at 1:00 pm. The leak was reported to have been stopped at its source.

MPA deployed seven craft equipped with dispersants, along with vessels from its contractor, Singapore Salvage Engineers. Shell also deployed additional craft and placed containment booms at the site to assist in cleaning up the oil.

In addition, MPA has also activated drones and satellite capabilities to assist with the detection of the oil spill. Relevant government agencies have been alerted to report any oil sightings. There have been no new oil sightings as of 6:00 pm.

Meanwhile, MPA’s Port Operations Control Centre stated that there is no impact on navigational safety. It has issued navigational safety broadcasts advising passing vessels to steer clear of the site.

data.title: Daily Markets Briefing: STI up 0.41%; Top stock is ST Engineering
data.description:
ST Engineering is the top stock with a 2.12% increase.

The Straits Times Index (STI) closed at 3,639.970 up 0.41% on Friday, 18 October, at 5:21 p.m.

ST Engineering is the most active stock, followed by CapLand IntCom T (+1.44%) and CityDev(+0.95%).

OCBC Bank and Frasers L&C Tr were at the foot.

data.title: COTD: Singapore leaders view GenAI as key to strategic leadership shifts
data.description:
Managers and leaders expect GenAI to improve their skills and productivity.

In Singapore, 65% of leaders and managers see strong potential in generative AI (GenAI) for complex strategic tasks, with over half believing managers will play a key role as catalysts for Gen AI-driven change, Capgemini reported.

In the next three years, 78% of leaders and managers expect Gen AI to enhance their problem-solving and decision-making abilities.

Meanwhile, 51% believe that many managerial roles will transition to either specialist or subject matter expert positions or top strategic leadership.

This sentiment is most prevalent amongst managers in the supply chain sector at 64%, followed by human resources at 63% and IT/engineering at 58%.

Similarly, 57% of managers in the product design/development and sustainability sectors share this sentiment.

Capgemini also found that GenAI could save leaders and managers up to seven hours each week, with nearly 8 in 10 leaders believing that Gen AI will positively impact their productivity in the next 12 months.

In addition, 57% of leaders of organisations advanced in their Gen AI implementation already see their roles becoming more strategic, whilst 97% of leaders and managers report having experimented with Gen AI tools. However, only 15% use them at least once a day in their work.

data.title: DBS unveils token services with smart contracts for transaction clients
data.description:
It makes use of DBS’ Ethereum Virtual machine-compatible permissioned blockchain.

DBS has introduced DBS Token Services, integrating tokenisation and smart contract-enabled capabilities for its transaction banking clients.

DBS Token Services offers instant and 24/7 real-time settlement of payments via blockchain. It makes use of DBS’ Ethereum Virtual Machine-compatible permissioned blockchain.

The smart contracts enable programmability for institutions to govern the use of funds according to predefined conditions.

data.title: Industrial landlords offer flexibility amidst softer demand
data.description:
Demand has shifted towards spaces capable of storing dangerous goods.

The industrial and logistics sector in Singapore remains healthy despite softer demand as landlords become more accommodating to secure tenants, according to CBRE’s Asia Pacific Industrial & Logistics Trends Q3 2024 report.

Larger third-party logistics firms are consolidating operations into flagship sites without expanding their overall footprint, leading to higher availability of smaller spaces in satellite locations.

Last year, planning for logistics facilities exceeding 100,000 sq ft required two years of lead time, but immediate requirements can now be met through active sourcing. CBRE expects this trend to continue until the end of 2025, offering cost-conscious occupiers new opportunities.

In recent quarters, demand has shifted towards spaces with technical specifications such as those capable of storing dangerous goods like petrochemicals. 

There has also been increasing interest from food occupiers seeking air-conditioned dry cargo spaces, along with a rising need for cold storage facilities. Meanwhile, e-commerce platforms are consolidating and seeking cheaper space.

CBRE also noted the growing interest in Johor Bahru, where companies are attracted by the availability of special economic and free trade zones. Its proximity to Singapore and lower costs make it a preferred location for businesses, with the Johor-Singapore Special Economic Zone agreement expected to be finalised by year-end.

Graeme Bolin, executive director of Industrial & Logistics Services at CBRE Singapore, expects landlords to offer more rent-free periods or fit-out incentives to attract tenants. He advised occupiers to take advantage of the current market conditions to secure attractive space, as occupancy rates and rents are likely to rebound to end-2023 levels within the next 12 to 15 months.

data.title: S.F. Express opens airside logistics centre at Changi Airfreight Centre
data.description:
The centre will reduce the company's on-airport processing time.

S.F. Express Singapore has opened its airside logistics centre at  SATS Core A warehouse in Changi Airfreight Centre.

The company said the centre will reduce its on-airport processing time from four to six hours to one and a half to two hours.

"The direct access to the airside enables the efficient transfer of cargo between the tarmac and the logistics centre," the company said.

"For imports, this access speeds up the process of getting goods from the terminal, allowing for faster sorting and distribution," the company added.

data.title: HDB rents rise 0.1% MoM and 3.7% YoY in September
data.description:
Volumes, however, fell.

HDB rents increased by 0.1% MoM and 3.7% YoY in September, 99.co and SRX reported.

Rents showed mixed movements across room types and estates on a sequential basis.

Mature estate rents dipped by 0.1%, whilst non-mature estate rents increased by 0.3%.

Meanwhile, 3-room, 4-room, and Executive rents increased by 0.3%, 0.2%, and 0.2%, respectively. Rents for 5-room decreased by 0.2%.

Year-on-year, however, rents increased for all room types and estates.

Rents increased by 3.7% YoY for 3-room, 4.2% YoY for 4-room, 2% YoY for 5-room, and 7% YoY for Executive.

Both Mature and Non-Mature Estate rents increased by 3.7%.

Rental volumes, on the other hand, fell in September, declining by 11.8% MoM and 17.3% YoY to 2,314 HDB flats. 

The latest record was also 11.7% lower than the five-year average volume for September.

Of the total volume,  32.2% was from 3-room, 38%  from 4-room,  24.2% from 5-room and  5.6% from Executive.

data.title: ESR-LOGOS secures $225m and JPY15.5b sustainability-linked term loans
data.description:
It will use proceeds from both facilities for corporate funding.

ESR-LOGOS has entered a $225m and JPY15.5b unsecured sustainability-linked loan facilities.

RHB Bank Berhad, and the Singapore branches of CIMB Bank Berhad, Malayan Banking Berhad, Sumitomo Mitsui Banking Corporation, and The Hongkong and Shanghai Banking Corporation Limited were the lenders of the $225m facility,

The JPY facility was secured with the Singapore branches of Malayan Banking Berhad and Mizuho Bank.

The REIT will use the proceeds from both facilities for general corporate funding purposes.
